title: EXCLUDE is not disallowed for framed window functions that don't support it
description:
### What happens?

All of the new non-aggregate window functions (except `dense_rank`) now use framing when an `ORDER BY` argument is provided, but they do not check whether unsupported `EXCLUDE` modifiers are present. 

### To Reproduce

The following queries should fail because the functionality is unsupported:

```sql
-- EXCLUDE in the OVER clause
SELECT 
	i,
	lead(i ORDER BY i // 2, i) OVER (
		ORDER BY i // 2
		ROWS BETWEEN 3 PRECEDING AND 3 FOLLOWING 
		EXCLUDE TIES
	) AS f,
FROM range(10) tbl(i)
ORDER BY i;

--EXCLUDE in a WINDOW clause
SELECT 
	i,
	lead(i ORDER BY i // 2, i) OVER w AS f,
FROM range(10) tbl(i)
WINDOW w AS (
		ORDER BY i // 2
		ROWS BETWEEN 3 PRECEDING AND 3 FOLLOWING 
		EXCLUDE TIES
	)
ORDER BY i;
```

This applies to all the new non-aggregate functions.

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.1.4-dev4931

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Richard Wesley

### Affiliation:

DuckDB Labs

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value FLOAT, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, name) VALUES (1, 10.5, 'Alice'), (2, 20.0, 'Bob'), (3, NULL, 'Charlie');
SELECT id, lead(value) OVER (ORDER BY id ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND 1 FOLLOWING) AS next_value FROM test_table;
SELECT id, lead(value) OVER w AS next_value FROM test_table WINDOW w AS (ORDER BY id ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND 1 FOLLOWING);
SELECT id, value, name FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC;
key_bug_features: Unsupported EXCLUDE modifiers in window functions
